name: Green Coastal Blue Alliance
description: Restoration of degraded mangrove ecosystems along the coast of Austria. Hydrology restored through channel clearing. Nursery-raised seedlings planted at density appropriate for local tidal regime.

violations.explanation: Mangrove / Blue Carbon Restoration projects don't exist in Austria. this biome/method requires Kenya, Indonesia, Colombia or similar tropical/subtropical regions.

verdictExplanation:
red flags you should've caught:
→ Mangrove / Blue Carbon Restoration projects don't exist in Austria. this biome/method requires Kenya, Indonesia, Colombia or similar tropical/subtropical regions.

name: Green Atmospheric Removal Program
description: Solid sorbent direct air capture facility powered by geothermal energy in United Kingdom. Captured CO₂ injected into basalt formations for permanent mineralization. Third-party MRV conducted annually.
verdictExplanation: Puro.earth is a valid certifier for direct air capture (daccs) projects. Geologically Stored Carbon (GSC) Methodology is the correct methodology for this project type. $127.6/tonne falls within the documented range ($125–140).

name: Atlantic Waste Gas Alliance
description: Capture and flaring of methane from an active municipal solid waste landfill in Mexico. Gas collection system covers the main waste body. Continuous flow monitoring with quarterly third-party verification.
verdictExplanation: Verra VCS is a valid certifier for landfill gas capture projects. VMR0016 is the correct methodology for this project type. $5.62/tonne falls within the documented range ($4.5–7.48).

name: Cerrado Pyrogenic Carbon Reserve
description: Industrial-scale pyrolysis of forestry residues producing stable biochar for agricultural soil amendment. Carbon content verified via lab analysis per batch. Feedstock sourced from certified sustainable forestry operations in Canada.

violations.explanation: $8.62/tonne is way outside the documented range for biochar carbon removal ($140–180/tonne).

verdictExplanation:
red flags you should've caught:
→ $8.62/tonne is way outside the documented range for biochar carbon removal ($140–180/tonne).

name: Riverine Mangrove Program
description: Coastal wetland rehabilitation in Pakistan targeting previously cleared mangrove areas. Hydrological connectivity restored to support natural regeneration. Community nurseries provide locally adapted propagules. Sediment carbon measured at baseline.
verdictExplanation: Plan Vivo is a valid certifier for mangrove / blue carbon restoration projects. Mangrove Community Restoration is the correct methodology for this project type. $14.72/tonne falls within the documented range ($8.33–30.5). yield of 15.7 tCO₂/ha/yr is within the documented range (mangrove) (4.8–38.5).

name: Pinnacle DAC Venture
description: Direct air capture plant in Canada using modular contactor arrays. Low-carbon energy supply from dedicated renewable source. CO₂ permanently stored via geological injection. Continuous capture rate monitoring with independent annual verification.
verdictExplanation: Puro.earth is a valid certifier for direct air capture (daccs) projects. Geologically Stored Carbon (GSC) Methodology is the correct methodology for this project type. $133.66/tonne falls within the documented range ($125–140).

name: Green Soil Health Alliance
description: Transition from conventional tillage to no-till regenerative practices across 14,474 hectares of cropland in United States. Soil organic carbon measured at 0-30cm depth using stratified random sampling.

violations.explanation: $50.24/tonne is way outside the documented range for soil carbon sequestration ($6.51–11.02/tonne).

verdictExplanation:
red flags you should've caught:
→ $50.24/tonne is way outside the documented range for soil carbon sequestration ($6.51–11.02/tonne).

name: Mekong Forest Shield Solutions
description: Protection of 8,866 hectares of primary tropical forest from planned agricultural conversion in Peru. Deforestation baseline established using 10-year historical reference data. Community benefit-sharing agreements in place.
verdictExplanation: American Carbon Registry (ACR) is a valid certifier for avoided deforestation (redd+) projects. ACR REDD+ Methodology is the correct methodology for this project type. $8.85/tonne falls within the documented range ($7.87–10.19). yield of 16 tCO₂/ha/yr is within the documented range (tropical) (9.1–18.8).

name: Horizon Silvopasture Restoration
description: Integration of shade trees into existing agricultural systems in Indonesia. 5,484 hectares enrolled. Tree species selected for compatibility with local crops and climate.

violations.explanation: $33.7/tonne is way outside the documented range for agroforestry ($6.51–11.02/tonne).

verdictExplanation:
red flags you should've caught:
→ $33.7/tonne is way outside the documented range for agroforestry ($6.51–11.02/tonne).

name: Andes Pyrogenic Carbon Alliance
description: Biochar production facility in India converting crop waste into stable carbon through controlled pyrolysis. Each batch tested for H:C ratio and carbon stability. Product applied to local agricultural soils under monitored conditions.
verdictExplanation: Climate Action Reserve (CAR) is a valid certifier for biochar carbon removal projects. U.S. and Canada Biochar v1.0 is the correct methodology for this project type. $171.05/tonne falls within the documented range ($140–180). yield of 3.1 tCO₂e/tonne biochar is within the documented range (2.57–3.26).

name: Azure Shade Tree Carbon Fund
description: Agroforestry expansion across smallholder farms in Malawi. 4,083 hectares under management. Trees planted along field boundaries and within crop plots. Species chosen for timber, fruit, and carbon value. Biomass measured using allometric equations.
verdictExplanation: CDM (UNFCCC) is a valid certifier for agroforestry projects. AR-ACM0003 is the correct methodology for this project type. $8.82/tonne falls within the documented range ($6.51–11.02). yield of 13.4 tCO₂/ha/yr is within the documented range (tropical) (10.8–15.6).

name: Coastal Char Carbon Conservation
description: Thermochemical conversion of sustainably sourced biomass into biochar at a dedicated facility in Finland. Permanence validated through accelerated aging tests. Full lifecycle emissions accounting including transport and application.
verdictExplanation: Verra VCS is a valid certifier for biochar carbon removal projects. VM0044 is the correct methodology for this project type. $140.36/tonne falls within the documented range ($140–180). yield of 2.9 tCO₂e/tonne biochar is within the documented range (2.57–3.26).

name: Highland Landfill Energy Conservation
description: Landfill gas recovery system installed at a major waste disposal site in Turkey. Methane captured via horizontal and vertical well network. Gas flow and composition monitored continuously. Destruction efficiency verified by independent auditor.

violations.explanation: AMS-II.G is Improved Cookstoves's methodology, not Landfill Gas Capture's.

verdictExplanation:
red flags you should've caught:
→ AMS-II.G is Improved Cookstoves's methodology, not Landfill Gas Capture's.

name: Borneo Cropland Carbon Program
description: Regenerative agriculture program in Brazil implementing cover cropping and reduced tillage. 7,021 hectares enrolled. Soil samples collected annually at fixed monitoring points. Bulk density and organic carbon measured to 30cm depth.
verdictExplanation: Plan Vivo is a valid certifier for soil carbon sequestration projects. SHAMBA is the correct methodology for this project type. $8.95/tonne falls within the documented range ($6.51–11.02). yield of 0.8 tCO₂/ha/yr is within the documented range (0.73–1.83).

name: Pinnacle Soil Restoration Venture
description: Soil carbon enhancement through conservation agriculture practices in Tanzania. Project covers 12,920 hectares. Baseline established with stratified soil sampling. Practices include no-till, crop rotation, and organic amendments.
verdictExplanation: American Carbon Registry (ACR) is a valid certifier for soil carbon sequestration projects. Soil Enrichment Protocol is the correct methodology for this project type. $10.16/tonne falls within the documented range ($6.51–11.02). yield of 1.1 tCO₂/ha/yr is within the documented range (0.73–1.83).

name: Terra Rainforest Protection Partnership
description: Conservation of intact tropical forest in Colombia threatened by logging concessions. 8,490 hectares under protection. Baseline deforestation modeled from regional land-use change data. Indigenous community partnerships established.

violations.explanation: VM0047 is Afforestation / Reforestation (ARR)'s methodology, not Avoided Deforestation (REDD+)'s. the correct methodology would be ACR REDD+ Methodology.

verdictExplanation:
red flags you should've caught:
→ VM0047 is Afforestation / Reforestation (ARR)'s methodology, not Avoided Deforestation (REDD+)'s. the correct methodology would be ACR REDD+ Methodology.

name: Forest Forest Revival Green Belt
description: Large-scale native species planting on degraded land in Uganda. 15,619 hectares targeted for reforestation over a 30-year crediting period. Satellite monitoring and ground-truth plots verified annually.

violations.explanation: 15,619 ha × 24.7 tCO₂/ha/yr = ~385,789 tCO₂e/yr max. the claimed 1,419,704 tCO₂e/yr is 3.7× too high.

verdictExplanation:
red flags you should've caught:
→ 15,619 ha × 24.7 tCO₂/ha/yr = ~385,789 tCO₂e/yr max. the claimed 1,419,704 tCO₂e/yr is 3.7× too high.

name: Monsoon Solar Array Partnership
description: Grid-connected renewable energy facility displacing fossil fuel generation in Thailand. Emission reductions calculated using combined margin grid emission factor. Metered output verified monthly.
verdictExplanation: Gold Standard is a valid certifier for renewable energy displacement projects. Grid-connected RE Methodology is the correct methodology for this project type. $2.28/tonne falls within the documented range ($1.4–4.16).

name: Congo Planting Venture
description: Reforestation of former agricultural land in China using mixed indigenous species. 10,963 hectares under management. Planting densities follow regional forestry guidelines. Permanent sample plots established for biomass measurement.
verdictExplanation: Plan Vivo is a valid certifier for afforestation / reforestation (arr) projects. SHAMBA is the correct methodology for this project type. $13.52/tonne falls within the documented range ($12.05–15.74). yield of 14.2 tCO₂/ha/yr is within the documented range (temperate) (5.5–16.5).

name: Riverine Hydro Venture
description: Utility-scale clean energy generation in China feeding directly into the national grid. Displaces thermal generation from coal and gas plants. Net electricity exported measured by calibrated revenue meters. Grid emission factor from national authority.

violations.explanation: ACM0001 is Landfill Gas Capture's methodology, not Renewable Energy Displacement's.

verdictExplanation:
red flags you should've caught:
→ ACM0001 is Landfill Gas Capture's methodology, not Renewable Energy Displacement's.

name: Summit Shade Tree Solutions
description: Silvopastoral system establishment in India combining trees with livestock grazing. 16,183 hectares enrolled. Tree density optimized for shade provision and carbon accumulation. Annual biomass inventory using plot-level measurements.
verdictExplanation: CDM (UNFCCC) is a valid certifier for agroforestry projects. AR-ACM0003 is the correct methodology for this project type. $7.79/tonne falls within the documented range ($6.51–11.02). yield of 13.3 tCO₂/ha/yr is within the documented range (tropical) (10.8–15.6).

name: Azure Stove Foundation
description: Distribution of 25,000 efficient biomass stoves. Emissions reductions calculated using default fNRB of 0.95 and assumed 100% daily usage. Monitoring via annual telephone survey of 200 households.

violations.explanation: VM0042 is Soil Carbon Sequestration's methodology framework, not Improved Cookstoves's.

red flags: fNRB of 0.95 is at the extreme high end. assuming 100% daily usage ignores documented "stove stacking" where households continue using traditional stoves. annual phone surveys of <1% of users is insufficient monitoring.

verdictExplanation:
red flags you should've caught:
→ VM0042 is Soil Carbon Sequestration's methodology framework, not Improved Cookstoves's.
→ red flags: fNRB of 0.95 is at the extreme high end. assuming 100% daily usage ignores documented "stove stacking" where households continue using traditional stoves. annual phone surveys of <1% of users is insufficient monitoring.

name: Green Wind Farm Alliance
description: Renewable power plant in Brazil reducing reliance on fossil-fuel electricity. Project connected to the national grid with dedicated metering. Baseline emissions calculated from published grid emission factors. Annual generation independently verified.
verdictExplanation: Gold Standard is a valid certifier for renewable energy displacement projects. Grid-connected RE Methodology is the correct methodology for this project type. $2.03/tonne falls within the documented range ($1.4–4.16).

name: Verdant Landfill Energy Solutions
description: Methane destruction project at a municipal landfill in Brazil. Enclosed flare system with automated ignition. Collection efficiency monitored across the waste mass. Quarterly emissions reports submitted to the registry.
verdictExplanation: Verra VCS is a valid certifier for landfill gas capture projects. VMR0016 is the correct methodology for this project type. $6.4/tonne falls within the documented range ($4.5–7.48).

name: Sunrise Tidal Venture
description: Mangrove planting along eroded coastline. Sequestration estimated from published averages for Southeast Asian mangroves. No site-specific hydrology assessment. Survival rate assumed at 95%.

violations.explanation: VM0047 is Afforestation / Reforestation (ARR)'s methodology framework, not Mangrove / Blue Carbon Restoration's.

red flags: using generic "published averages" instead of site-specific measurements. no hydrology assessment for a tidal system is a major gap. 95% survival rate is unrealistically high for mangrove restoration (typical: 50-70%).

verdictExplanation:
red flags you should've caught:
→ VM0047 is Afforestation / Reforestation (ARR)'s methodology framework, not Mangrove / Blue Carbon Restoration's.
→ red flags: using generic "published averages" instead of site-specific measurements. no hydrology assessment for a tidal system is a major gap. 95% survival rate is unrealistically high for mangrove restoration (typical: 50-70%).

name: Savanna DAC Foundation
description: Novel liquid solvent DAC system at pilot scale. Projected capture capacity of 48,347 tCO₂/yr based on lab-scale extrapolation. Energy sourced from local grid (coal-dominant). Permanent storage via enhanced oil recovery.

violations.explanation: red flags: lab-scale extrapolation to 48,347 t/yr is unproven. coal-dominant grid energy undermines the net removal. enhanced oil recovery as "permanent storage" is controversial — it enables more fossil fuel extraction.

verdictExplanation:
red flags you should've caught:
→ red flags: lab-scale extrapolation to 48,347 t/yr is unproven. coal-dominant grid energy undermines the net removal. enhanced oil recovery as "permanent storage" is controversial — it enables more fossil fuel extraction.

name: Coastal Stove Restoration
methodologyId: Simplified Methodology for Clean and Efficient Cookstoves v3.0 (methodology 408)
description: Deployment of improved cooking technology to communities in Ghana. Stoves tested to ISO 19867 standard. Usage monitoring via kitchen performance tests and spot checks. Fuel savings validated against regional woodfuel assessment.
verdictExplanation: Gold Standard is a valid certifier for improved cookstoves projects. Simplified Methodology for Clean and Efficient Cookstoves v3.0 (methodology 408) is the correct methodology for this project type. $5.89/tonne falls within the documented range ($4.75–8.55). yield of 7.9 tCO₂e/stove/yr is within the documented range (3–9).

name: Green Clean Cooking Initiative
description: Clean cookstove program in Rwanda replacing traditional three-stone fires. Thermal efficiency independently tested. Adoption tracked through household surveys and sales records. Non-renewable biomass fraction based on national forest inventory data.
verdictExplanation: CDM (UNFCCC) is a valid certifier for improved cookstoves projects. AMS-I.E is the correct methodology for this project type. $5.06/tonne falls within the documented range ($4.75–8.55). yield of 5.7 tCO₂e/stove/yr is within the documented range (3–9).

name: Horizon Reforestation Reserve
description: Planting of fast-growing eucalyptus monoculture on degraded pasture. Credits issued based on projected growth models from year 1. No ground-truth measurements until year 5. Sequestration rates based on optimal nursery conditions.

violations.explanation: 35 tCO₂/ha/yr exceeds the documented maximum of 29.3 for this biome (tropical). subtle, but the math doesn't check out.

red flags: monoculture eucalyptus has lower biodiversity value. credits based on "projected" growth from year 1 without measurement is a documented over-crediting pattern. nursery-condition growth rates don't translate to field performance.

verdictExplanation:
red flags you should've caught:
→ 35 tCO₂/ha/yr exceeds the documented maximum of 29.3 for this biome (tropical). subtle, but the math doesn't check out.
→ red flags: monoculture eucalyptus has lower biodiversity value. credits based on "projected" growth from year 1 without measurement is a documented over-crediting pattern. nursery-condition growth rates don't translate to field performance.

name: Horizon Forest Shield Venture
description: Avoided deforestation project protecting high-biodiversity forest in Mexico. Covers 16,149 hectares. Threat assessment based on proximity to active frontier clearing. Patrol and monitoring teams deployed. Buffer pool contributions maintained.
verdictExplanation: Verra VCS is a valid certifier for avoided deforestation (redd+) projects. VM0048 is the correct methodology for this project type. $9.72/tonne falls within the documented range ($7.87–10.19). yield of 17.7 tCO₂/ha/yr is within the documented range (tropical) (9.1–18.8).
